segments.text: "Then David asked,
Is there anyone left from the house of Saul to whom I can show kindness for the sake of Jonathan?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "And there was a servant of Saul’s family named Ziba. They summoned him to David, and the king inquired,
Are you Ziba?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am your servant,
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: he replied.
"So the king asked,
Is there anyone left of Saul’s family to whom I can show the kindness of God?
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: Ziba answered,
There is still Jonathan’s son, who is lame in both feet.
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: Where is he?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text:
replied the king.
And Ziba said,
Indeed, he is in Lo-debar at the house of Machir son of Ammiel.
segments.speaker: Ziba

segments.text: "So King David had him brought from the house of Machir son of Ammiel in Lo-debar.

"And when Mephibosheth[^39] son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, came to David, he fell facedown in reverence.
Then David said,
Mephibosheth!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am your servant,
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth

segments.text: he replied.
Do not be afraid,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: said David,
for surely I will show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan. I will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul, and you will always eat at my table.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "Mephibosheth bowed down and said,
What is your servant, that you should show regard for a dead dog like me?
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: "Then the king summoned Saul’s servant Ziba and said to him,
I have given to your master’s grandson all that belonged to Saul and to all his house.
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: You and your sons and servants are to work the ground for him and bring in the harvest, so that your master’s grandson may have food to eat. But Mephibosheth, your master’s grandson, is always to eat at my table.
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.
"And Ziba said to the king,
Your servant will do all that my lord the king has commanded.
segments.speaker: Mephibosheth bowed down and

segments.text: So Mephibosheth ate at David’s table[^40] like one of the king’s own sons.
"And Mephibosheth had a young son named Mica,[^41] and all who dwelt in the house of Ziba were servants of Mephibosheth.
"So Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, because he always ate at the king’s table, and he was lame in both feet.
